summarization.a: The figure shows the XRD pattern of a platinum film grown at 200 °C, highlighting a strong (111) peak, indicating that the film has a preferred (111) crystal orientation, with smaller peaks corresponding to (200), (220), and (311) planes.

d.question: How does the preferred (111) orientation of the platinum film influence its properties?
d.answer: The (111) orientation corresponds to the lowest surface energy, which makes the film more thermodynamically stable. This orientation contributes to better adhesion on substrates such as Al2O3 and iridium and supports low electrical resistivity, enhancing the performance of the platinum film for electronic applications.

b.question: Which crystallographic plane shows the strongest peak in the XRD pattern of the platinum film?
